conjecture_title: Informal Conjecture (Equality in Theorem 1.1 near the zero-rate threshold)
paper_title: Set-coloring Ramsey numbers and error-correcting codes near the zero-rate threshold
summary: No follow-up work proving or disproving the conjectured equality R(q+1;r,s) = A_q(r,s)+1 near the zero-rate threshold was found. The source paper was subsequently published in IEEE Transactions on Information Theory (2024). A related paper by Aragão, Collares, Marciano, Martins, and Morris (Random Structures & Algorithms, 2024) studies lower bounds for set-coloring Ramsey numbers in a different parameter regime (fixed clique size, varying r and s) and does not address the equality conjecture in the zero-rate threshold setting.
search_queries: set-coloring Ramsey numbers error-correcting codes zero-rate threshold Conlon Fox Pham Zhao 2305.14132
R(q+1;r,s) A_q(r,s) set-coloring Ramsey equality lower bound sharp
notes: Wide search across arXiv, Semantic Scholar, Conlon's publication page, and PubMed returned no evidence of a paper resolving the equality conjecture. The conjecture is ~3 years old; open with high confidence. A related paper by Aragão et al. (PMC10952192, RSA 2024) addresses a different parameterization and is not a follow-up to this specific conjecture.
